name: query_board_profile
description: Fetch the selected board profile before proposing pins or code.

name: search_packages
description: Search MicroPython packages by query, capabilities, and board.

name: resolve_package_candidates
description: Resolve package candidates for a hardware intent and inferred capabilities.

name: get_package_context
description: Fetch machine-readable driver context for a resolved package.

name: propose_manifest
description: Propose a hardware manifest with selected board, packages, pins, logic, and wiring.

manifest.description: Hardware manifest. pins maps each ROLE to a board pin string (role -> pin); do not invert it.

board_id.description: Board id from query_board_profile, e.g. esp32-s3-devkitc-1.

driver_context_refs.description: Refs to fetched driver contexts, e.g. "aht20_driver@1.0.0".

pins.description: Object mapping each ROLE to a board PIN string (role -> pin), NOT pin -> role and NOT role -> object. Use roles from the driver pin_roles and pins allowed by the board profile's pin_capabilities / pin_recommendations. Example: {"i2c_sda":"GPIO5","i2c_scl":"GPIO6","led_anode":"GPIO2"}.

wiring.description: Device-identity wiring (subset of the upstream wiring.schema). buses[] = shared comm buses, each with signals[] (role SDA/SCL/... -> gpio) and the devices[] on that bus (name + type + I2C addr); standalone[] = direct-GPIO parts (LED/buzzer/button), each {name, pin, type}. Derive it from pins: ONE physical part = exactly ONE device or standalone entry. Never emit a bare {role, pin} list. The wiring diagram renders from this.

summary.description: Short friendly explanation shown to the user as the build plan.

name: generate_code
description: Generate MicroPython project code from a validated hardware manifest.

name: audit_code
description: Audit generated MicroPython code before writing or flashing it.

name: load_skill
description: Load a MicroPython hardware skill body by name.

name: ask_user
description: Ask the user a question through the interactive UI.

name: install_package
description: Install a resolved MicroPython package onto the selected device.

name: flash_and_run
description: Reset or run the generated program on the selected MicroPython device.

name: read_serial_until
description: Read serial output until expected marker strings are observed.

name: write_main_py
description: Write generated main.py content to the selected MicroPython device.

name: read_workspace_file
description: Read a file from the user's project workspace (path relative to the workspace root) to inform code generation or normalization.

name: write_project_file
description: Write a file into the user's project workspace (path relative to the workspace root). Use for the project manifest and the firmware project tree, e.g. project-manifest.json, firmware/main.py, firmware/conf.py, firmware/drivers/<name>/__init__.py, firmware/drivers/<name>/mock.py, firmware/tasks/<task>.py, test/pc/test_<task>.py. The host enforces path containment and an allowed-path set; paths outside it are rejected.

path.description: Workspace-relative path, for example firmware/tasks/sensor.py or project-manifest.json.

name: run_validate
description: Validate a project JSON file against the canonical upstream schema (project-manifest, wiring, or diagram) by running the vendored validate_json.py on the host. Returns whether it is valid plus the schema error messages. The schema is the single source of truth — use this as the authoritative gate after writing the manifest or wiring/diagram JSON.

schema.description: Which canonical schema to validate against.

path.description: Project-relative path of the JSON file to validate (default project-manifest.json).

name: run_scaffold
description: Generate the deterministic firmware/ project skeleton (board.py, conf.py, boot.py, main.py template, lib/logger, lib/scheduler, tasks/maintenance, driver stubs, tools/, README, .flake8) by running the vendored init_scaffold.py on the host. Requires a project-manifest.json at the select-hw phase (with mcu, pinout, devices). Writes files directly into the project directory.

mode.description: Scheduler mode for the generated main.py (default timer).

name: run_download_drivers
description: Download the device drivers listed in project-manifest.json (devices[].driver) from upypi/GitHub into firmware/lib, along with README and example references, by running the vendored download_drivers.py on the host. Network access required.
